documentation.value: Describe the element as a goal: a high-level end state or outcome that stakeholders intend to achieve. In this architecture, Operationalize Structured Threat-Intel Analysis defines, constrains, or supports traceable solution intent within the managed model. Deliver a minimal but runnable threat-intelligence analysis loop that can accept OPENCTI PUSH events, orchestrate remote evidence-driven analysis, and produce reusable structured outputs for downstream security operations.

The repository's core capability is to automate the intake, normalization, remote orchestration, evidence correlation, and structured reporting steps required to analyze pushed threat-intelligence events.

Describe the element as a course of action: an approach, directive, or plan for configuring capabilities and resources. In this architecture, Minimal Closed-Loop Delivery defines, constrains, or supports traceable solution intent within the managed model. Implement the smallest executable end-to-end slice by keeping the Python listener thin, delegating heavy analysis to the remote OPENCODE workspace, and preserving local mocks and validation assets for regression testing.

Describe the element as a business actor: an organizational entity that is capable of performing behavior and owning or using business roles, processes, or services. In this architecture, Threat Intelligence Analyst / SecOps Operator defines, constrains, or supports traceable solution intent within the managed model. A threat-intelligence or security-operations practitioner who consumes pushed indicators, needs evidence-backed triage conclusions, and uses the system's output to inform containment, hunting, and follow-up investigation.

Describe the element as a business process: a sequence of business behavior that produces a defined set of business outcomes. In this architecture, Threat Push Analysis Flow defines, constrains, or supports traceable solution intent within the managed model. Receive an OPENCTI PUSH event, normalize its STIX-relevant context, submit it to remote multi-agent analysis, review the evidence-backed verdict and recommendations, and retain the resulting artifact for validation or operational use.

Describe the element as a business service: externally visible business behavior delivered to customers or other business roles. In this architecture, Structured Threat Triage Service defines, constrains, or supports traceable solution intent within the managed model. Provide analysts with a structured threat-analysis result that summarizes the pushed event, captures supporting STIX evidence, states the confidence and verdict, and recommends operational actions in a machine-readable format.

Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Core Application System is the modular software building block that automates and supports the business process behind Current Product Goal.

Automated application service that accepts normalized threat-event context, coordinates remote evidence-backed analysis, and returns a structured threat-triage result for analyst consumption and downstream validation.

Describe the element as a data object: passive data suitable for automated processing by application behavior. In this architecture, Core Domain Data captures the domain and state information created, read, and maintained by the core application system.

Describe the element as a node: a computational or physical resource that hosts, executes, or supports technology and application behavior. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime is the runtime environment that hosts and executes the technology and application behavior required for Current Product Goal.

Describe the element as an artifact: a physical or deployable piece of information used or produced in technology or application realization. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the deployable solution package generated from the core application system for operation on the target runtime node.

Ingress application component that receives or reads mock OPENCTI PUSH events, normalizes the inbound context, resolves the main remote agent, dispatches the request to the remote OPENCODE SERVER over the session/message protocol, and persists the structured JSON analysis artifact under artifacts/runtime/.

Input-normalization component that validates the mock OPENCTI PUSH payload contract, trims and canonicalizes entity and observable fields, and converts raw event JSON into the normalized event structure consumed by the listener and downstream analysis flow.

Result-contract component that builds, validates, and guards the TASK-009 style structured threat-analysis JSON payload, combining event context, evidence summaries, collaboration trace data, and recommended actions into a listener-consumable result.

Protocol-compatible remote-analysis component used for local validation. It emulates the OPENCODE SERVER session/message boundary, derives STIX evidence from the sample bundle, synthesizes multi-role collaboration output, and returns a schema-aligned structured response.

Local STIX query component that loads STIX 2.1 bundles, searches entities by textual indicators and names, and expands relationship neighborhoods for evidence inspection used by the mock remote orchestration path and repository validation.

[Input]: File path or CLI argument pointing to a mock OPENCTI PUSH event JSON payload; optional output path; optional remote server URL and main-agent override.
[Processing]: The listener loads the event file, invokes the mock OPENCTI adapter to normalize entity, observables, labels, and severity fields, creates a run context with run_id and timestamps, and prepares the normalized payload for remote analysis dispatch.
[Output]: A normalized event dictionary and run context ready for remote request assembly.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Valid event payloads are accepted and normalized; invalid contract data is rejected before remote dispatch; each run produces a stable event_id-linked run context.

[Input]: Normalized event context, resolved main agent, remote server base URL, and TASK-009 JSON schema contract.
[Processing]: The listener builds a remote request embedding request context and prompt text, creates a remote session via POST /session, submits the message via POST /session/{sessionID}/message, and validates returned JSON candidates against the structured-result contract.
[Output]: One validated structured threat-analysis result from the remote OPENCODE SERVER boundary.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The session/message protocol is used in order; remote transport and malformed-response failures surface as dispatch errors; only schema-valid structured results are accepted.

[Input]: Valid structured analysis result plus target output path or default artifacts/runtime/<event_id>-analysis.json destination.
[Processing]: The listener resolves the destination path, creates parent directories as needed, serializes the structured result as UTF-8 JSON, and writes the artifact to the runtime output area.
[Output]: A persisted JSON analysis artifact on disk and the same structured result returned to the caller.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Output directories are created automatically; written JSON preserves non-ASCII content; default filenames remain event_id-based when no explicit path is supplied.

[Input]: Raw mock OPENCTI event JSON object containing contract_version, event metadata, entity object, observables, labels, and severity.
[Processing]: The adapter checks that required fields are present and non-empty, verifies that entity and observables use the expected object and array shapes, and raises contract errors when the inbound payload violates the declared minimal contract.
[Output]: Confirmed contract-valid raw event fields suitable for canonical normalization, or an explicit EventContractError.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Missing entity data, empty required strings, or absent observables are rejected; labels may be optional but must be arrays when present; contract enforcement happens before normalization output is produced.

[Input]: Contract-valid raw event object with nested entity and observable records.
[Processing]: The adapter trims strings, converts entity and observable payloads into typed dataclass-backed structures, filters labels to non-empty strings, and emits a normalized event object with a stable dictionary representation.
[Output]: A NormalizedMockOpenCTIEvent structure and equivalent dictionary payload for downstream listener use.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Whitespace-only values are removed or rejected according to field criticality; output preserves entity, observables, labels, severity, and source fields in normalized form; to_dict returns a downstream-consumable structure.

[Input]: Run context, normalized event, evidence bundle, and collaboration output from remote primary, analyst, and SecOps roles.
[Processing]: The result assembler combines event facts, evidence statistics, final assessment data, recommended actions, and collaboration trace fields into the threat-intelligence-agent.v1 structured result document aligned to the TASK-009 assembly contract.
[Output]: A complete structured analysis result JSON object ready for validation and listener-side persistence.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Required top-level fields are populated; collaboration trace contains participant and assembly information; analysis conclusion and key information summary are derived from supplied evidence and role outputs.

[Input]: Candidate structured result object generated locally or returned remotely.
[Processing]: The result assembler checks the presence of mandatory top-level fields, verifies that at least two analysis participants are recorded, and exposes the canonical JSON schema used for remote response formatting.
[Output]: Confirmation that the result satisfies the minimal structured-result contract, or a ValueError describing the missing or invalid contract condition.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Results missing required fields are rejected; collaboration traces with fewer than two participants are rejected; the published JSON schema remains available for remote message formatting.

[Input]: HTTP POST requests to /session and /session/{sessionID}/message, plus mock remote server state and request payloads.
[Processing]: The mock remote server creates transient session identifiers, stores captured requests, routes message calls only for known sessions, and returns protocol-compatible response envelopes for local validation.
[Output]: Session creation responses, message responses, and an in-memory trace of captured remote requests.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unknown endpoints or unknown sessions return errors; valid session creation produces a reusable session id; message responses retain the protocol shape expected by the listener client.

[Input]: Normalized event containing entity name and observables plus a local STIX bundle path.
[Processing]: The mock remote orchestration flow loads the STIX bundle, searches by entity and observable terms, selects matched STIX object ids, expands neighbor relationships for leading candidates, and packages searches and relationship views into a single evidence bundle.
[Output]: An evidence bundle containing searched terms, match counts, matched entities, and related relationship views.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Entity and observable values are both used as search seeds; duplicate candidate ids are avoided; resulting bundle includes both search summaries and relationship context when available.

[Input]: Main agent name, normalized event, and STIX evidence bundle collected during remote mock analysis.
[Processing]: The mock orchestration flow canonicalizes legacy role names, interprets matched entities and relationships, derives verdict and confidence signals from evidence text, constructs analyst and SecOps role outputs, and prepares the final assessment metadata consumed by the result assembler.
[Output]: Collaboration output containing participant roles, traceability, final assessment summary, verdict, confidence, supporting entities, and recommended actions.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Canonical and legacy role traces are both preserved; verdicts remain evidence-grounded; the final assessment contains enough data for TASK-009 style result assembly.

[Input]: File path to a local STIX 2.1 JSON bundle.
[Processing]: The STIX utility reads the JSON file, confirms the top-level object is a STIX bundle, and ensures an objects collection exists for downstream semantic queries.
[Output]: An in-memory STIX bundle dictionary suitable for search and relationship traversal.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Non-bundle inputs are rejected; valid bundle files load with UTF-8 decoding; downstream callers can rely on an objects array being present.

[Input]: Loaded STIX bundle and a query string derived from a threat entity name or observable value.
[Processing]: The STIX utility performs case-insensitive text matching across type, name, description, pattern, and value fields for each STIX object and summarizes matched objects into lightweight evidence records.
[Output]: Search result payload containing the original query, total match_count, and summarized matching STIX entities.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Matching is case-insensitive; empty or whitespace-only queries do not generate false-positive matches; returned summaries include ids, types, names or values, and available descriptive evidence.

[Input]: Loaded STIX bundle and a target STIX object id.
[Processing]: The STIX utility locates the referenced object, traverses relationship objects where the target is source or peer, labels each relation as incoming or outgoing, and summarizes connected peer objects for evidence context.
[Output]: A neighborhood view containing the focal STIX object, relationship_count, and related peer summaries.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Missing STIX ids are rejected explicitly; only STIX relationship objects are traversed; returned neighborhood data preserves direction, relationship type, and peer identity context.

Remote agent-workspace component that defines the canonical primary, analyst, and SecOps roles, declares the collaboration boundary and role aliases, supplies OPENCODE configuration for the remote execution plane, and anchors the threat-intelligence orchestration contract executed by the external OPENCODE SERVER.

[Input]: Remote workspace configuration files, canonical agent definitions, and legacy alias names used by older tests and prompts.
[Processing]: The workspace configuration declares canonical remote roles, maps legacy agent names onto current role names, and exposes the default primary agent used by listener-side dispatch.
[Output]: A stable remote role model with canonical and legacy-compatible agent naming.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Canonical roles remain ThreatIntelPrimary, ThreatIntelAnalyst, and ThreatIntelSecOps; legacy aliases continue to resolve without breaking validation assets; the default main agent is discoverable from workspace configuration.

[Input]: Threat-intelligence push-analysis request context, role permissions, and the declared TASK-009 result obligation for the remote workspace.
[Processing]: The remote workspace documents the primary-to-analyst-to-SecOps delegation order, constrains which role may perform STIX querying, and defines the structure of analyst, SecOps, and final primary outputs.
[Output]: A documented remote orchestration contract that governs multi-agent collaboration and final result assembly.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Delegation order remains Primary -> Analyst -> SecOps -> Primary; only the analyst role is permitted to use STIX query capability; final output requirements stay aligned with the TASK-009 structured result contract.
Current OpenCode project goal.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Product Capability makes Product Delivery Goal concrete by providing the ability needed to achieve that strategic outcome.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, MVP Delivery Course Of Action gives concrete form to Core Product Capability by prescribing how that capability will be established.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow operationalizes Core Product Capability through executable business behavior.

Describe the relationship as an assignment: the allocation of responsibility, behavior, or execution from an active structure element to a behavior element, or vice versa where appropriate in ArchiMate. In this architecture, Primary User is allocated participation responsibility in Core Business Flow from a business perspective.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow implements and makes Core User Service concrete as externally visible behavior.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core User Service provides business value and externally visible behavior to Primary User.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Application Service provides the automated support needed by the business-facing Core User Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Application System implements and makes Core Application Service concrete as the exposed application service boundary.

Describe the relationship as an access relationship: the source behavior or active structure concept reads, writes, or otherwise uses the target passive structure concept. In this architecture, Core Application System reads and writes Core Domain Data in order to execute application behavior.

Describe the relationship as an association: a generic structural or semantic link used when no more specific ArchiMate relationship type applies. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime has a structural support link with Core Application System where no more specific baseline relationship is modeled.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime provides the infrastructure behavior required to host and expose Core Application Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the concrete deployable manifestation of Core Application System.
The Python Listener component is responsible for ingesting and preparing inbound PUSH events for the remote analysis workflow.
The Python Listener component owns remote session/message dispatch and acceptance of the structured analysis response.
The Python Listener component persists the final structured analysis artifact into the runtime output directory.
The Mock OPENCTI Event Adapter is responsible for enforcing the inbound event contract before downstream processing.
The Mock OPENCTI Event Adapter produces the canonical normalized event structure consumed by the listener.
The Structured Result Assembler composes the final TASK-009 aligned analysis document.
The Structured Result Assembler guards the result contract and rejects incomplete outputs.
The Remote Analysis Orchestrator Mock owns the protocol-compatible session/message handling behavior.
The Remote Analysis Orchestrator Mock derives STIX evidence and relationship context for the analysis workflow.
The Remote Analysis Orchestrator Mock composes the multi-role assessment and final verdict metadata.
The STIX Semantic Query Utility loads local STIX bundle data for later evidence operations.
The STIX Semantic Query Utility performs textual evidence searches across STIX objects.
The STIX Semantic Query Utility traverses STIX relationship neighborhoods for evidence context.
The Remote Threat Intel Workspace is responsible for canonical role naming and compatibility alias definitions used by remote dispatch.
The Remote Threat Intel Workspace owns the multi-agent collaboration contract executed by the remote OPENCODE SERVER.

name.value: Listener serves threat push analysis flow

documentation.value: The Python Listener provides the automated intake and dispatch behavior needed to execute the threat push analysis flow.

name.value: Adapter serves threat push analysis flow

documentation.value: The Mock OPENCTI Event Adapter supports the business flow by validating and normalizing incoming threat-event payloads.
The Remote Analysis Orchestrator Mock provides the automated collaboration and evidence synthesis behavior behind the structured threat triage service.
The Structured Result Assembler supports the business service by producing the machine-readable threat-triage output consumed by analysts.
The STIX Semantic Query Utility supports the business flow by supplying evidence lookup and relationship expansion behavior.
The Remote Threat Intel Workspace provides the configured remote collaboration boundary that delivers the structured threat triage service.
The Python Listener is part of the concrete implementation of the remote threat analysis service.
The Remote Analysis Orchestrator Mock implements core remote-analysis behavior of the remote threat analysis service.
The Structured Result Assembler implements the output-contract portion of the remote threat analysis service.
The Remote Threat Intel Workspace realizes the configured remote orchestration portion of the remote threat analysis service.
Core layered intention model maintained by SystemArchitect.
